description: baseline: pread path, K=10, 100 tokens, correct output
static GCD pread queue + USER_INTERACTIVE QOS (no improvement)
F_RDADVISE speculative next-layer prefetch (massive regression, I/O contention)
matvec_4bit_2row for pread expert+shared down projections (within noise, GPU not bottleneck)

per-layer fused experts for resident layers in hybrid mode (neutral, resident layers not bottleneck)
fix: pure pread for 397B (restore baseline from partial mlock regression)

sorted expert indices by ID before pread (within noise, GCD concurrent anyway)

dynamic expert pruning (min weight threshold): weights too evenly distributed (min ~3-5%), no experts pruned
mmap without mlock + MADV_RANDOM for non-resident layers (massive regression, page fault overhead)
fcntl(F_RDAHEAD,0) disable read-ahead on expert layer files (regression, read-ahead helps page cache)

2-bit K=8: 20% fewer expert reads, quality remains coherent (but deviates from K=10 architecture)
dispatch_apply for pread (cleanup): within noise of dispatch_group baseline
re-implemented I/O instrumentation: 2-bit io=92-101ms/tok bw=25GB/s 78% preads 200us-1ms
conditional instrumentation (zero-cost when not profiling): within noise
parallel pread+CPU expert forward (eliminate GPU cmd buffer): CPU matvec 20x slower than GPU
dynamic expert pruning 5% threshold: weights too even, ~1 layer pruned per token, within noise

concurrent GPU dispatch (MTLDispatchTypeConcurrent) for expert forward: +7% from better GPU utilization

expand cache to 2×K slots with proper full-width search
